contradiction_text: Different frameworks lead different tasks, so the evaluation does not support a single best framework across code-centric software engineering work.
related_claim_texts: The evaluation finds no single agent framework that leads across all three code-centric tasks, because different frameworks post the top reported results in software development, vulnerability detection, and program repair.
disclosure: Keep visible that benchmark leadership changes by task and that the evidence supports only benchmark-bounded cross-task comparison, not a universal winner claim.

contradiction_text: OpenHands leads overall software-development quality, but the strongest component metrics are split across frameworks rather than concentrated in one system.
related_claim_texts: In the software-development benchmark, OpenHands leads the overall quality score at 0.47, but the strongest submetrics are split across frameworks rather than concentrated in one system.
disclosure: Keep visible that overall quality leadership does not mean dominance across completeness, executability, and consistency.

contradiction_text: GPTswarm posts the best overall vulnerability-detection result, yet Gas Limitation vulnerabilities remain rarely detected across the evaluated agents.
related_claim_texts: GPTswarm posts the strongest reported vulnerability-detection result at 80 of 115 cases, but Gas Limitation vulnerabilities remain rarely detected across the evaluated agents.
disclosure: Keep visible that top aggregate detection performance does not remove a shared category-level weakness in Gas Limitation cases.

contradiction_text: SE-Agent (Iter-3) leads overall program repair, but no evaluated agent repairs any of the three Flask issues in the benchmark.
related_claim_texts: In program repair, patch-tooling capability appears to matter materially, because frameworks reported as lacking patch tooling and correct diff-format patch generation perform poorly while SE-Agent (Iter-3) leads with 161 of 300 repairs.
disclosure: Keep visible that even the leading repair result remains bounded by a complete failure on at least one repository slice, so aggregate repair performance should not be read as broad repair robustness.

contradiction_text: Efficiency and capability do not align cleanly, because GPTswarm is the lowest-cost framework while also showing poor repair effectiveness tied to missing patch-tooling capability.
related_claim_texts: In program repair, patch-tooling capability appears to matter materially, because frameworks reported as lacking patch tooling and correct diff-format patch generation perform poorly while SE-Agent (Iter-3) leads with 161 of 300 repairs.
disclosure: Keep visible that low total cost should not be treated as a general competence signal across tasks.

contradiction_text: OpenHands uses the most tokens across tasks, but it is not the most expensive framework because cached input tokens are cheaper.
related_claim_texts: In the software-development benchmark, OpenHands leads the overall quality score at 0.47, but the strongest submetrics are split across frameworks rather than concentrated in one system.
disclosure: Keep visible that token volume and dollar cost diverge here because the pricing result is conditioned by cached-input-token economics.
